key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share

key_assumptions.source: Base component sum $179/sh versus price; terminal value equals normalized component equity
component base sum from proof-complete schedule

open_questions: Confirm post-refresh 10-K extract matches locked FY2025 facts in `evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-15.json`.
Product-level capacity/import map remains a bounded stress reserve, not a full industry database.
thesis: Nucor Corporation is the largest steel producer in the United States by tonnage, built almost entirely on electric-arc-furnace (EAF) mini-mills fed by scrap and direct-reduced iron. **[Fact]** The company reports three operating segments: steel mills (sheet, plate, bar, and structural products), steel products (joist, deck, rebar fabrication, towers, and other downstream businesses), and raw materials (scrap yards, DRI, and brokerage). **[Fact]** FY2025 pretax segment earnings were **$2.383 billion** for mills, **$1.229 billion** for products, and **$153 million** for raw materials, with corporate eliminations of **$1.197 billion**. **[Fact]** Steel-mill shipments were **25.271 million tons** at **83%** capacity utilization. **[Fact]** Nucor is also in the late stages of a multi-year capital program, including a West Virginia sheet mill targeted at roughly **3 million tons** of annual capacity for about **$3.65 billion** net of state incentives. **[Fact]**
why_market_wrong: We do not see a durable mispricing signal at today's price. **[Inference]** Nucor's cost position, balance-sheet strength, and project pipeline may justify a premium to our through-cycle component base of about **$179 per share** versus a price near **$231**. **[Inference]** The market may be capitalizing mid-cycle spreads and successful project completion, while our base case normalizes 2025 peak mill utilization and keeps an explicit supply-response reserve. **[Inference]** If projects earn above their cost of capital and industry supply discipline holds, the bull component schedule (**~$334 per share**) could prove conservative; if imports and new domestic capacity depress spreads, the low case (**~$68 per share**) is the relevant stress path.
